categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue and EPS figures, profit margins, guidance, dividend announcements, buyback programs, and market cap milestones.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, Nasdaq/S&P 500 movements, macro data reactions, tech sector trends, sector rotation, and articles where GOOGL is mentioned as part of a wider market or tech sector narrative.

categories.label: Analyst Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell/hold ratings, broker commentary, institutional positioning changes, and valuation milestone commentary.

categories.label: Antitrust, Regulatory & Legal
categories.description: DOJ search monopoly case, EU fines and DMA investigations, UK CMA probes, Play Store rulings, ad tech trials, privacy lawsuits, potential forced breakup, and global regulatory enforcement actions.

categories.label: AI & Product Strategy
categories.description: Gemini model launches and controversies, AlphaFold breakthroughs, custom chip development (Trillium, Axion), AI search features, DeepMind research, Google I/O announcements, Pixel hardware releases, and AI partnerships.

categories.label: Cloud & Infrastructure Investment
categories.description: Data center construction globally, cloud partnership deals with enterprise clients, nuclear and renewable energy procurement, clean energy agreements, submarine cable projects, and multi-billion dollar regional infrastructure commitments.

categories.label: Acquisitions & Strategic Investments
categories.description: Major acquisition targets (Wiz, HubSpot), talent acquisitions (Character.AI), minority investments (Flipkart, Anthropic), licensing deals with AI startups, divestitures, and portfolio adjustments.

categories.label: Leadership, Workforce & Corporate
categories.description: C-suite appointments and departures, layoffs and restructuring, organizational changes, Waymo autonomous vehicle developments, corporate governance actions, and operational policy shifts.

categories.label: Advertising & Revenue Ecosystem
categories.description: Ad tech practices and disputes, third-party cookie deprecation, ad sales restructuring, YouTube monetization, publisher licensing conflicts, and Google Play store policies.

dimensions.scale: 1=trivial mention in a roundup or minor operational detail with no revenue or margin implications, 10=directly and substantially impacts core revenue streams, margins, or total addressable market
1=outcome fully expected or already priced into consensus expectations, 10=outcome completely unexpected with no prior indication or dramatically divergent from expectations
1=affects only the next few trading days with no lasting structural consequence, 10=structural shift affecting the company's trajectory for years
1=neutral or balanced tone with no clear positive or negative valence, 10=extremely strong positive or negative tone throughout the article
1=pure opinion, speculation, or vague commentary with no specific data, 10=rich in hard quantitative data including specific financial figures, deal values, and measurable outcomes
1=ambiguous implications that could reasonably be interpreted as positive or negative, 10=unambiguously good or bad with clear directional impact on the company
1=affects only this company with no broader implications, 5=sector-wide implications for big tech, 10=macro or market-wide event affecting all equities
1=no effect on competitive positioning relative to peers, 10=fundamentally reshapes the competitive landscape in a key business segment
1=no regulatory, legal, or government enforcement angle, 10=existential regulatory threat such as forced structural separation or business-line divestiture
1=transient story that resolves or is forgotten within a single news cycle, 10=persistent story remaining relevant for weeks or months with ongoing developments and follow-up coverage
1=reinforces the prevailing investment thesis and existing market narrative, 10=fundamentally challenges or reframes the core investment thesis around the company
1=rehash or incremental update of a previously well-reported story with no new information, 10=first report of genuinely new information not previously in the public domain

1=no financial results reported or results exactly in line with prior guidance and consensus expectations, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss analyst expectations as described in the article

kept.materiality: All 3 runs kept. Ranges from passing mentions in market roundups to DOJ breakup proposals and $23 billion acquisition talks that directly affect enterprise value, revenue, and margins.
kept.surprise: All 3 runs kept. Ranges from pre-announced earnings dates and expected filings to the unexpected DOJ breakup proposal, Gemini image generation bias controversy, surprise first-ever dividend, and sudden Wiz acquisition talks.
kept.temporal_horizon: All 3 runs kept. Ranges from single-day market roundups and minor fines to multi-year antitrust proceedings, decade-long nuclear energy partnerships, and structural AI investment commitments.
kept.sentiment_strength: All 3 runs kept. Ranges from neutral partnership announcements to strongly positive earnings beats with first-ever dividend and $2 trillion valuation, and strongly negative DOJ breakup threats and billion-euro fines.
kept.information_density: All 3 runs kept. Ranges from one-line trending alerts and opinion pieces to detailed earnings releases with specific revenue, EPS, margin, and segment breakdowns plus deal values in the billions.
kept.directional_clarity: All 3 runs kept. Ranges from ambiguous events where competitive benefit and regulatory risk coexist to unambiguous earnings beats, clear fine amounts, and definitive court rulings.
kept.scope: All 3 runs kept. Ranges from company-only events like CFO appointment and Waymo incidents to sector-wide DMA investigations affecting all Big Tech and macro-level Fed policy or global tax negotiations.
kept.competitive_impact: All 3 runs kept. Ranges from routine enterprise partnerships to Gemini launch directly competing with OpenAI, Character.AI talent acquisition in the AI race, DOJ search remedies threatening Google's core moat, and nuclear energy deals reshaping data center economics.
kept.regulatory_risk: All 3 runs kept. Dominant theme for GOOGL with massive variation from zero-regulatory product launches to DOJ seeking full corporate breakup, EU upholding $2.7 billion fine, UK CMA findings, and simultaneous DMA non-compliance investigations across multiple jurisdictions.
kept.expected_duration: All 3 runs kept. Ranges from one-day market roundup mentions to the DOJ antitrust saga, EU DMA investigations, and recurring Waymo safety concerns that generate continuous coverage spanning the entire corpus period.
kept.narrative_shift: All 3 runs kept. Ranges from data center investments reinforcing the established AI-capex narrative to the DOJ breakup proposal and federal monopoly ruling which fundamentally altered the risk profile and reframed GOOGL as facing structural regulatory risk.
kept.repeatedness: All 3 runs kept. High variation between first-break stories like Wiz acquisition talks, Gemini launch, and CFO hiring versus fourth or fifth updates on the same EU fine or antitrust ruling within hours, and multiple wire duplicates of press releases.
kept.financial_result_surprise: All 3 runs kept as custom dimension. Critical for distinguishing between earnings events across four quarterly cycles: Q4 2023 disappointed on cloud growth causing a sharp selloff, Q1 2024 beat with 61% EPS growth triggering first-ever dividend, and Q3 2024 beat on cloud acceleration — while the majority of non-earnings articles score at the floor.

dropped.management_signal: Kept by Run 1 only; dropped by Runs 2 and 3. Would score uniformly low for approximately 90% of articles since most coverage involves regulatory, product, or market events rather than leadership decisions. The few leadership articles are adequately captured by the leadership_workforce_corporate category and materiality dimension.
dropped.controversy: Kept by Run 3 only; dropped by Runs 1 and 2. GOOGL articles rarely show genuine investor division; antitrust risk and AI competition are broadly agreed-upon concerns where investors differ on magnitude rather than direction, and the concept is already partially captured by regulatory_risk and directional_clarity.
dropped.actionability: Dropped by all 3 runs. For a mega-cap stock like GOOGL with deep liquidity, nearly all substantive news is immediately actionable, producing insufficient variation across the corpus to be discriminating.
